title: Inventory sequencing violations and lifecycle flow
summary: Sequencing enforcement is centered in state_store.py (tx_event invariants), state_rebuilder.py (rebuild halts on invalid ordering), ops_tools.py (ops_start_task/ops_update_task), and commit_manager.py (commit_if_verified). Reported errors map to invariant checks for missing verify.start, active-tx gating on tx.begin, and tx_id mismatches during updates.
acceptance_criteria: All tool sequencing touchpoints in src/ are enumerated with file locations.
Transaction lifecycle flow (start/update/verify/commit) is documented with current ordering.
Each reported error case is mapped to a specific code path and missing precondition.

steps.description: Locate all tools and helpers that emit or validate transaction events and lifecycle state.
Trace verify/commit/task flows to capture current ordering and missing guardrails.
Map the three reported errors to concrete code paths and required ordering fixes.

notes: Focus on sequencing and transaction state validation only; avoid unrelated refactors.

Error mapping: commit_if_verified -> verify result requires verify.start (state_store); ops_start_task -> tx.begin rejected when active_tx not terminal + event log non-empty; ops_update_task -> tx_id mismatch when active_tx is stale due to rebuild stopping on invalid event ordering.
